title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Quin and Professor Adler review investigative reports concerning the deaths of Andrew Van and Thomas Braid. An autopsy of Andrew Van yielded no significant findings, and a district attorney's investigation concluded there was no direct relationship between Van and the murdered millionaire Thomas Braid, despite similarities in their past correspondence. Dr. Rumsen’s report on Braid indicates no signs of violence, poisoning, or asphyxiation, suggesting the cause of death was a gunshot or a blunt object to the head.

The investigation into the killer's escape route proved unsuccessful, as police could not identify any suspicious travelers by train, car, or boat in the Gulf of Ketchum on the Tuesday of the crime. Regarding the totem pole where Braid was found, the ropes used to bind him were identified as ordinary clotheslines, and experts noted the knots were clumsy but effective. Additionally, the background of a man who joined an Egyptology group in Pittsburgh remains unknown.

Ellery discusses the suspicious Rein couple, Percy and Elizabeth, noting that Scotland Yard has been unable to find matching records despite their claims of British citizenship. Further findings reveal that a woman resembling Mrs. Braid left the Park Theatre alone on Tuesday night. A deposit check for renting Oyster Island was written by Vilga Croxall in Peoria, Illinois. While the Brad-Megal Company appears financially stable, Ellery notes that Stephen Megalos has been idle at sea for five years. Finally, Ellery discovers a suppressed report regarding Vilga Krossak, revealing that Krossak was an immigrant from Montenegro who arrived in America around the time of the Tutankhamun tomb incident.
